title: MATRIYA Capability Ontology
description: STABLE v1.0. The shared vocabulary that joins what TASKS consume to what ENGINES provide. Engines self-declare a capability vector over 7 PRIMITIVES (identical to the frozen Engine Contract v1.0 capability axes). Tasks may reference primitives OR derived capabilities; the Capability Planner expands derived -> primitives to match engines. The Planner therefore selects engines by capability, never by name. Documentation only; no runtime behaviour.

primitives.description: The atomic capabilities. MUST be exactly the 7 Engine Contract v1.0 capability-vector axes — this identity is what makes the join total.

engineAxis.description: True: engines self-rate this axis 0..5 in their contract.

derived.description: Compound capabilities tasks may reference. Each expands to a set/sequence of primitives; engines do NOT self-declare these — the Planner resolves them.

rule.description: How the primitives combine (e.g. 'observe over >=2 subjects, then explain differences').

taskTypes.description: The kinds of scientific task a user goal resolves to. Each carries a typical capability flow (ordered, primitive or derived ids).

typicalFlow.description: Ordered capability ids (primitive or derived) that this task type usually needs.
